summary: Justin Brennan mentors on analyzing a 27-unit value-add multifamily deal in Colleen, Texas. The group walks through underwriting adjustments including rent comps verification, expense reconciliation, DCR improvement strategies, and capital structure optimization using a deal analyzer tool.
takeaways: Use AI/ChatGPT to verify property taxes by address: input purchase price and get assessed value projections (expect ~90% of purchase price initial assessment)
1970s multifamily buildings should operate at 45-55% expense ratio; anything above 55% signals bad operations or cost overruns requiring detailed investigation
Maintain 3% annual rent growth assumptions in proformas; brokers tend to overestimate—use 10% economic loss (vacancy/bad debt/concessions) minimum in Texas markets, not 5-8%
Separate bank accounts by function: operating account (daily rents/expenses), CapEx account (renovations/big ticket items), entity account (distributions)—prevents forensic accounting problems
For DCR below acceptable threshold, options are: increase down payment (35-45%), reduce purchase price, cut expenses, or refinance at year 3 to improve cash flow profile

Always include 'other income' line items even if broker OM shows zero: late fees, admin fees, application fees, pet rent, parking fees, utility reimbursements typically add $5k+ annually on 27-unit property
Submit LOI even when numbers are close but not perfect; use LOI process to build broker relationships and negotiate expense details rather than rejecting deals outright

skill_candidates.change: Systematically verify broker-provided expense figures against market benchmarks using expense ratio guidelines before accepting underwriting
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin identifies 1970 property expenses running 57%+ when 45-55% is target range; teaches to hover over analyzer fields for benchmarks; stresses 'you got bad operations' if expenses exceed 55%

skill_candidates.change: Cross-check rent growth assumptions and cap rate inputs using AI tools and public tax assessor data rather than accepting broker estimates
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin uses ChatGPT/AI to input property address and purchase price to pull assessed values and future tax projections; corrects Marco's 6.5% cap rate assumption and verifies rent comps against Rentometer

skill_candidates.change: Adjust economic loss (vacancy/bad debt) to market-specific minimums: 10% minimum for Texas/Midwest, only 5-8% in top coastal markets
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin states 'there's just no way around it' for 10% in Midwest; explicitly warns against brokers underwriting at 5% when realistic numbers require 10%; notes Huntsville AL is rare exception due to rocket industry concentration

skill_candidates.change: Use DCR constraint as pricing lever: if DCR fails at asking price, back into acceptable down payment % rather than rejecting deal
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin shows 0.94 DCR is unacceptable; calculates backward that 35-40% down payment gets deal to acceptable DCR range; tests 45% down initially, then optimizes to 35% when expenses are corrected

title: Tuesday Mentorship Q&A - Real Estate Deal Breakdown_04/21/26 | Justin Brennan
